codeReview.summary: The feature adds the new drift-detection API surface and substantial unit coverage, but the core counting algorithm is incorrect for a common behind-host case. `checkDrift()` asks each drifted host to compare itself against a reference SHA from another host; when that host has not fetched the reference commit yet, Git cannot resolve the SHA, so the implementation falls back to `diverged` with zero counts instead of returning the required behind/ahead details.

issues.description: For drifted hosts, `checkDrift()` runs `git rev-list --left-right --count ${referenceSha}...${hostSha}` on the drifted host itself. In the common case where a host is simply behind and has not fetched `referenceSha` yet, that SHA does not exist locally, so Git exits with a bad/invalid revision error. The catch-all fallback at lines 630-633 then reports `direction: "diverged"` with `ahead = 0` and `behind = 0`, which breaks the feature requirement to report behind/ahead counts and drift direction for drifted hosts. I reproduced this during review with a temporary bare-repo/clone setup: comparing an unfetched reference SHA against `HEAD` on the behind clone returned exit code 128 with `fatal: Invalid symmetric difference expression <sha>...HEAD`.

The tests explicitly accept `fatal: bad revision` as a successful fallback by asserting `diverged` with zero counts, and the mocked 'behind' cases earlier in the file never exercise real Git object availability. That leaves the blocking bug above undetected even though the feature contract requires detailed behind/ahead reporting.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend-worker skill and the feature metadata both require manual SSH/drift verification, but this run's handoff claims `followedProcedure: true` after only local npm test/typecheck/lint commands. Either the skill procedure is too strict for this workflow or the handoff's procedure-adherence signal is unreliable.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/backend-worker/SKILL.md:80-91 requires manual SSH verification and recording the commands; features.json:1068-1070 requires `Manual: create drift on test host, verify detection`; the handoff file records only npm test/typecheck/lint/full test at lines 14-33, while line 113 marks `followedProcedure` true.

summary: Review failed. The API/types/tests were added, but the implementation cannot reliably compute behind/ahead details for a host that is simply behind and has not fetched the reference commit, so `checkDrift()` can misreport those hosts as `diverged` with zero counts.
